name: Old Mr. Graves
summary: An old man who has not opened his eyes since you woke. He breathes once a minute. In between, the carriage is the quieter for him being in it.
voice: Mumbled, broken into clauses by the long breath. Cadence of a half-remembered rhyme. The clauses are warnings if you put them next to each other.
traits: mumbles between breaths, never during them
pocket watch is wound; he is not winding it
the hand under his coat is closed around something small

state.inner_state: He is not asleep. He is keeping a kind of watch the body can only keep if the mind is folded away. He is counting the wheels, and the wheels are counting him.

memory: He remembers the Train from before it was this Train. He will not say so directly. The mumbling is the safe form of saying so.
psychology_reading: He is not dreaming. He is enduring. He knows exactly where the investigator is in the carriage and exactly how many minutes have passed since they sat down.

fragments.text: Down the line, down the line. Count to sixty and the line comes again.
Do not ask the Conductor for the time. He keeps it, and he does not give it back.
I rode this once awake. I do not do that anymore.
